content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Contextualization: Dynamic Prompt-Guided Defense for Robust LLMs",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for LLM robustness rely on static, human-written instructions or system prompts that can be circumvented by sophisticated adversarial attacks or jailbreak attempts.",
  "motivation": "LLMs are increasingly deployed in high-stakes applications where adversarial misuse poses significant risks. Existing prompt-based defenses often fail when attackers exploit model weaknesses, and the static nature of these prompts leaves systems vulnerable to prompt injection or context manipulation. There is a pressing need for adaptive, context-aware prompting techniques that can anticipate and neutralize attack strategies in real-time.",
  "method": "We propose a dynamic prompting approach where an auxiliary classifier continuously monitors incoming user prompts and recent conversational context to detect potential adversarial intent. Upon suspicion, the system automatically injects adversarial-contextualized sub-prompts—tailored challenge questions or clarifying statements—designed to elicit further information from the user and disrupt known jailbreak patterns. The sub-prompts are selected or synthesized by a prompt generator module, conditioned on the detected attack vector and recent conversation, to adaptively steer the LLM away from unsafe completions. This method augments standard system prompts, providing a real-time, context-driven defensive layer.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on jailbreak and adversarial prompt datasets (e.g., AdvBench, JailbreakBench) using state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4, Llama-2-Chat). Baselines include static system prompts and recent red-teaming prompt strategies. Metrics will include successful attack rate, safe completion rate, and false positive user disruption. Ablation studies will assess classifier and prompt generator contributions."
}
